name: Camino del Cid from Covarrubias — circular through the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park - La Yecla
name_original: Camino del Cid desde Covarrubias — circular por el Parque Natural Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla
region_intro: Jogging routes around Covarrubias are set within the picturesque Arlanza river valley, characterized by extensively wooded areas, including Spanish Juniper. The region encompasses parts of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park, offering varied terrain from gentle riverside paths to routes with inclines. This landscape provides diverse options for running, from valley floors to higher elevations. Best jogging routes around Covarrubias The most popular jogging route is Camino del Cid from Covarrubias — circular through the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park - La Yecla, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging run through the natural park's diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is San Clemente - Mecerreyes Loop, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This route features rolling terrain and scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Local runners also love the Historic Center of Covarrubias – Casa de Doña Sancha loop from Covarrubias, a 14.7 miles (23.7 km) trail leading through the historic village and its environs, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Jogging around Covarrubias is defined by the Arlanza river valley, dense woodlands, and the varied terrain of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more difficult trails with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Covarrubias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Covarrubias?
A: There are over 8 dedicated running routes around Covarrubias, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are well-documented on komoot, with contributions from over 30 local runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Covarrubias?
A: The jogging trails in Covarrubias offer a diverse landscape. You'll find routes winding through the picturesque Arlanza river valley, characterized by extensively wooded areas, including Spanish Juniper. Many trails also venture into the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park, providing varied terrain from gentle riverside paths to more challenging routes with significant inclines.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Covarrubias?
A: While many routes in Covarrubias are rated moderate to difficult due to the varied terrain and elevation changes, there are options that can be adapted for beginners. For example, parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid loop from Barrio del Arrabal&lt;/a&gt; offer a more manageable experience with less elevation gain, making it suitable for those new to running in the area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running in Covarrubias?
A: Jogging in Covarrubias allows you to experience both natural beauty and historical charm. You might encounter the unique Spanish Juniper trees in the Arlanza river valley, or explore parts of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park. For historical sights, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4659990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; itself is a Property of Cultural Interest, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Doña Sancha&lt;/a&gt; is another historical site you could pass. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, a famous cinematographic location, is also within reach.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Covarrubias offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050096144/camino-del-cid-from-covarrubias-circular-through-the-sabinares-de-arlanza-natural-park-la-yecla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid from Covarrubias — circular through the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park - La Yecla&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging run through diverse landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050108949/san-clemente-mecerreyes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Clemente - Mecerreyes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, known for its rolling terrain and scenic views.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while jogging in Covarrubias?
A: Yes, the region around Covarrubias is a Special Protection Area for bird-life, including vultures. Running through the extensively wooded Arlanza river valley and the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park offers good opportunities for wildlife observation, especially for birdwatching.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Covarrubias?
A: Covarrubias offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. While winter can be cooler, the natural beauty of the Arlanza river valley and the natural park can still be enjoyed, though some higher elevation trails might be more challenging.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by a waterfall?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a route that includes a waterfall, consider exploring trails that might lead you towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6062888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Churrión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. While not directly on every jogging route, it's a notable natural monument in the broader area that can be incorporated into a longer run or visited as a highlight.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly good for families?
A: For families, routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances are generally preferred. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, consider exploring the flatter sections along the Arlanza river valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid loop from Barrio del Arrabal&lt;/a&gt;, being a moderate route, could be a good option for active families, allowing for a pleasant experience without overly strenuous climbs.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Covarrubias?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse and scenic landscapes of Covarrubias. The combination of the tranquil Arlanza river valley, dense woodlands, and the varied terrain of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park offers a rich and engaging experience. The historical charm of the village itself, often integrated into routes, also adds a unique cultural dimension to the physical activity.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views from higher elevations?
A: Absolutely. The region includes parts of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park, which features Alto de Peña Alta, rising to 1070 meters above sea level. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34337280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hortigüela – Castillejo loop from Hortigüela&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult route with significant elevation gain, will reward you with expansive views of bluish peaks and the beautiful Mediterranean landscape from higher vantage points.

Q: Are there options for longer distance running in Covarrubias?
A: Yes, Covarrubias offers several routes suitable for longer distance running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias – Casa de Doña Sancha loop from Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 14.7-mile (23.7 km) trail, often completed in about 3 hours, providing a good challenge for endurance runners. Other difficult routes also offer considerable distances and elevation.

Be careful, we are in a natural space, there is a ban on approaching the bird nesting area.
Road that goes from the town of Retuerta in the direction of Covarrubias and is called Camino del Cid. It moves along the Río Arlanza.

waypoints.name: Landscape on the Camino del Cid
